+# Execution Report
+
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-144
+AUTHOR: Executor / Claude
+TIMESTAMP: 2026-05-24_10-28-13+0300
+RELATED_FILES:
+- Wait-GitHubActions.ps1 — request `workflowName` from `gh run list --json`; match explicit `-WorkflowName` values against either the run's `workflowName` field or its display `name` field while preserving exact-name matching, branch/commit filtering, latest-`createdAt` selection, default workflow discovery, timeout enforcement, and exit-code meanings.
+STATUS: AWAITING_REVIEW
+
+## Task Packet Reference
+
+`ai_handoffs/ISSUE-144_TASK_2026-05-24_10-15-45+0300.md`
+
+## Pre-Execution Review Consumed
+
+No pre-execution review issued for this dispatch.
+
+## What I Changed
+
+### Source
+
+- `Wait-GitHubActions.ps1`: in the polling loop, added `workflowName` to the `--json` field list passed to `gh run list`, hoisted the createdAt-descending sort of branch+commit-filtered runs into `$sortedMatchingRuns`, and replaced the per-expected-name `$latestByName` hashtable lookup with a `Where-Object { $_.workflowName -eq $name -or $_.name -eq $name } | Select-Object -First 1` over the sorted list. The default-workflow case (no `-WorkflowName`) still builds `$latestByName` keyed by `$run.name` and derives `$namesToCheck` from it, so default workflow discovery, sort order, allowed-success set, deadline-then-sleep ordering, and exit-code semantics are unchanged.
+
+### Tests
+
+- None. The task explicitly scopes deliverables to `Wait-GitHubActions.ps1` and forbids adding new test, source, workflow, or doc files. Verification is the acceptance command below, run against live GitHub Actions state.
+
+### Docs
+
+- None. The task forbids adding doc, ADR, status, or planning files; the existing comment-based `.SYNOPSIS`/`.PARAMETER WorkflowName` block already documents that `-WorkflowName` accepts arbitrary names beyond the local-workflow defaults, which remains accurate.
+
+## Per-File Summary
+
+`Wait-GitHubActions.ps1` (single hunk in the `while ($true)` loop):
+
+- `--json` field list now: `databaseId,name,workflowName,status,conclusion,headSha,createdAt,url`.
+- `$sortedMatchingRuns = @($matchingRuns | Sort-Object @{ Expression = { [datetime]$_.createdAt }; Descending = $true })` runs once per poll; `$latestByName` is then populated from `$sortedMatchingRuns` rather than re-sorting inline. This preserves the "latest run wins" rule used for default `namesToCheck` discovery.
+- Per expected name (whether explicit `-WorkflowName` values or default-discovered workflow names), the resolved run is `$sortedMatchingRuns | Where-Object { $_.workflowName -eq $name -or $_.name -eq $name } | Select-Object -First 1`. Because `$sortedMatchingRuns` is already createdAt-descending, `-First 1` is the newest run matching either field — preserving "latest run per expected workflow" while extending matching to the workflow-identity field.
+- No fuzzy, substring, or explicitly case-folded matching introduced. `-eq` and hashtable lookups retain PowerShell's default string comparison semantics, matching the pre-change behavior on the `name` field.
+- Display row `Name` column still shows `$run.name` when a run is found, mirroring the prior output; `missing` rows fall back to the requested expected name.
+- Branch filtering (`gh run list --branch $Branch`), commit filtering (`$_.headSha -eq $Commit`), allowed-success set (`success`, `skipped`, `neutral`), deadline check before each poll and before each sleep, sleep cap to remaining time, and exit codes (0 / 1 / 2) are untouched.
+
+## Verification Results
+
+Pre-edit baseline:
+
+- `git status --short --untracked-files=no` → empty output (no tracked changes). Untracked items present (informational only, untouched by this dispatch): `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-144_TASK_2026-05-24_10-15-45+0300.md` and its `.meta.json` sidecar.
+
+Post-edit diff inspection:
+
+- `git diff -- Wait-GitHubActions.ps1` shows the `--json` argument now requests `workflowName` (line 171) and the per-name lookup uses `Where-Object { $_.workflowName -eq $name -or $_.name -eq $name } | Select-Object -First 1` (lines 191–193). Both Verification-Gate inspections are satisfied.
+
+Live acceptance command (run after the in-progress CodeQL run on the target commit completed with `success`):
+
+- `.\Wait-GitHubActions.ps1 -Repo RustCADs/RGE -Branch main -Commit 42b2bceb980c552c3e7c705e99fe3bc4ec79acea -WorkflowName CodeQL -TimeoutMinutes 2 -PollSeconds 5` → exit `0`. Output reported a single row `Name=Push on main Status=completed Conclusion=success RunId=26354873461` and `All expected GitHub Actions completed successfully.`, confirming that a run whose display `name` is `Push on main` but whose `workflowName` is `CodeQL` is accepted by the explicit `-WorkflowName CodeQL` expectation.
+
+  Note for the Reviewer: an earlier execution of the same command at 10:24 local exited `2` (TIMEOUT) because run `26354873461` was still `in_progress` (CodeQL Advanced workflow on commit `42b2bce…` had started at `2026-05-24T07:13:54Z`). I polled `gh run view 26354873461 ...` until it reported `completed:success`, then re-ran the acceptance command and it exited `0`. The waiter matched the run via `workflowName` in both runs of the command, which is the behavior change under test; the only reason the first invocation failed was live CI state, not the new matching logic.
+
+Post-edit / post-EXEC-report worktree:
+
+- `git status --short --untracked-files=no` → ` M Wait-GitHubActions.ps1` only. Tracked diff is limited to `Wait-GitHubActions.ps1`. Untracked items remain limited to the prior `ISSUE-144_TASK_*` packet and its sidecar plus the newly scaffolded `ISSUE-144_EXEC_2026-05-24_10-28-13+0300.md` (this report) — all permitted by the TASK scope.
+
+No cargo, formatter, architecture-lint, or workspace test command was required by this TASK and none was run.
+
+## Deviations from Task Packet
+
+None — execution stayed strictly within the Task Packet scope. The single tracked edit is `Wait-GitHubActions.ps1`; no files outside the `MAY edit` / `MAY add new files` envelope were created or modified, no `.github/**` file was touched, and `CodeQL` was not added to the default expected workflow list.
+
+## Open Questions for Reviewer
+
+- The acceptance command is inherently coupled to live GitHub Actions state on commit `42b2bce…`. If the Reviewer re-runs it and a *new* CodeQL run has since started on the same commit and is still pending, the command can again exit `2` purely due to in-flight CI — not the matching logic. The matching logic itself (resolving `-WorkflowName CodeQL` to the run whose `workflowName == 'CodeQL'`) is independently observable in the table emitted by the waiter on each poll, even before the run completes. No code change required either way.
+- I deliberately retained the `$latestByName` hashtable keyed only by `$run.name` for the default (no `-WorkflowName`) case, so the row list and "names observed for this commit" presentation is byte-for-byte identical to the pre-change behavior when expectations are not supplied. If the Reviewer would prefer the default case to also surface `workflowName`-only workflows that have no matching `name` collision, that is a follow-up Planner decision rather than something to expand here.
+
+## Worktree State
+
+- Tracked files: `M Wait-GitHubActions.ps1` (single modified file, uncommitted, as required by the no-commit protocol rule).
+- Untracked items: `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-144_TASK_2026-05-24_10-15-45+0300.md`, `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-144_TASK_2026-05-24_10-15-45+0300.meta.json`, `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-144_EXEC_2026-05-24_10-28-13+0300.md` (this report). No `ai_dispatch_logs/log_*.md` artifact created by the Executor; if the queue runner produces one, it is permitted by the TASK.
+- Branch: `ai-dispatch/ISSUE-144`.
+- Last commit: `42b2bce` chore(dispatch): seed task #30 Wait-GitHubActions CodeQL matching.
+
+---
+
+HANDOFF_STATUS: COMPLETE
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-144
+AUTHOR: Executor / Claude
+NEXT_ROLE: REVIEWER_AI
+EXIT_CODE: 0
+
+---

@@ -0,0 +1,122 @@
+# Task Packet
+
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-144
+AUTHOR: Planner / OpenAI Codex
+TIMESTAMP: 2026-05-24_10-15-45+0300
+RELATED_FILES:
+- Wait-GitHubActions.ps1
+- .github/workflows/*.yml
+- AI_DISPATCH_AUTOMATION.md
+- ai_handoffs/AI_HANDOFF_PROTOCOL.md
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-144_EXEC_*.md
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-144_EXEC_*.meta.json
+- ai_dispatch_logs/log_*.md
+- GitHub issue #144
+STATUS: OPEN
+
+## Goal
+
+Fix GitHub issue #144 by updating `Wait-GitHubActions.ps1` so an explicit workflow expectation such as `-WorkflowName CodeQL` can match a GitHub Actions run whose workflow identity is reported in the `workflowName` JSON field, while preserving the existing default workflow list, branch and commit filtering, latest-run selection, timeout behavior, and exit-code meanings.
+
+## Scope
+
+### MAY edit
+- `Wait-GitHubActions.ps1`
+
+### MUST NOT edit
+- Any file under `.github/**`, including workflow files.
+- Any Rust source, tests, Cargo file, generated Cargo metadata, dependency declaration, fixture, generated asset, schema, architecture lint, documentation file, status/planning file, ADR, or repository-root script other than `Wait-GitHubActions.ps1`.
+- Any existing handoff packet or existing dispatch log, including this TASK packet after execution begins.
+- Any GitHub issue, label, PR, branch, review, comment, commit, remote state, or queue metadata except the queue runner's normal issue lifecycle for this dispatch.
+
+### MAY add new files
+- Exactly one execution report matching `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-144_EXEC_*.md`.
+- The matching `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-144_EXEC_*.meta.json` sidecar only if `new-handoff.ps1 -Finalize` creates it while finalizing the EXEC report.
+- This dispatch's own queue-runner log under `ai_dispatch_logs/log_*.md` only if the queue runner creates it as part of normal dispatch operation.
+
+### MUST NOT add new files
+- New source files, test files, workflow files, docs, fixtures, generated assets, Cargo files, dependency files, schemas, scripts, architecture lints, TASK packets, REVIEW packets, CLOSEOUT packets, issue notes, or design documents.
+- Any handoff packet other than this dispatch's single `ISSUE-144_EXEC_*.md` report and optional matching `.meta.json` sidecar.
+
+## Deliverables
+
+- `Wait-GitHubActions.ps1` includes `workflowName` in the `gh run list --json` field list.
+- Explicit expected workflow names match a run when the expected value equals either the run's `workflowName` field or the run's `name` field.
+- The implementation preserves exact-name matching semantics; do not introduce fuzzy, substring, or case-folded matching.
+- Latest-run selection is still by newest `createdAt` among runs already filtered to the requested branch and commit SHA.
+- Default expected workflows are still derived only from local `.github/workflows/*.yml` and `.github/workflows/*.yaml` `name:` values when `-WorkflowName` is not supplied.
+- `CodeQL` is not added to the default expected workflow list unless a future tracked workflow file in `.github/workflows/` declares that name.
+- One `EXECUTION_REPORT` packet documents the implementation, verification commands, exact exit codes, and before/after worktree status.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- `.\Wait-GitHubActions.ps1 -Repo RustCADs/RGE -Branch main -Commit 42b2bceb980c552c3e7c705e99fe3bc4ec79acea -WorkflowName CodeQL -TimeoutMinutes 2 -PollSeconds 5` exits 0 on the current green `main` commit.
+- A CodeQL run whose `workflowName` is `CodeQL` is accepted even if its `name` field is not exactly `CodeQL`.
+- Existing default behavior is preserved: without `-WorkflowName`, expected workflows are read from `.github/workflows/*.yml` and `.github/workflows/*.yaml` only. At planning time those tracked defaults are `Architecture lints`, `Script bench compile`, `Supply chain (cargo-deny)`, `Format check`, and `Workspace tests`; `CodeQL` must not appear by default.
+- Branch filtering remains delegated to `gh run list --branch $Branch`, and commit filtering remains `headSha -eq $Commit`.
+- The waiter still keeps only the latest relevant run per expected workflow before evaluating status and conclusion.
+- Exit-code meanings remain unchanged: 0 means all expected workflows completed with an allowed conclusion, 1 means at least one expected workflow completed with a failing conclusion, and 2 means timeout before all expected workflows reached a terminal successful state.
+- Timeout enforcement remains unchanged: the script checks the deadline before each poll and before each sleep, and caps sleep to the remaining time.
+- The final tracked diff is limited to `Wait-GitHubActions.ps1` plus this dispatch's own `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-144_*` and queue-runner log artifacts.
+
+## Constraints / Non-Goals
+
+- Do not edit workflow files or add CodeQL to `.github/workflows/`.
+- Do not add CodeQL, `Push on main`, or any repository-level external workflow to the default expected workflow list by hard-coding it in the script.
+- Do not change the default workflow discovery function except as needed to preserve its current behavior.
+- Do not change `Repo`, `Commit`, `Branch`, `TimeoutMinutes`, `PollSeconds`, or `Limit` parameter behavior except where the implementation must read `workflowName`.
+- Do not change the allowed-success conclusion set of `success`, `skipped`, and `neutral`.
+- Do not alter command-line UX beyond making explicit `-WorkflowName` match either `workflowName` or `name`.
+- Do not commit, push, open a PR, create or edit GitHub issues, change labels, or write follow-up packets.
+
+## Verification Gates
+
+The Executor MUST run and document the result of each of these in the `EXECUTION_REPORT`:
+
+- `git status --short --untracked-files=no` before any edit. Document all visible tracked changes. Do not clean, revert, or modify unrelated user changes.
+- Inspect the final `Wait-GitHubActions.ps1` diff and document that `gh run list --json` requests `workflowName`.
+- Inspect the final `Wait-GitHubActions.ps1` diff and document that expected workflow matching checks both `workflowName` and `name`.
+- `.\Wait-GitHubActions.ps1 -Repo RustCADs/RGE -Branch main -Commit 42b2bceb980c552c3e7c705e99fe3bc4ec79acea -WorkflowName CodeQL -TimeoutMinutes 2 -PollSeconds 5` with expected exit code 0.
+- `git status --short --untracked-files=no` after implementation and after writing the EXEC report. Confirm that visible tracked changes are limited to `Wait-GitHubActions.ps1`, this dispatch's own `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-144_*` packet artifacts, and this dispatch's own `ai_dispatch_logs/log_*.md` artifact.
+
+No cargo, Rust formatter, architecture-lint, or workspace test command is required by this TASK. If the orchestrator or queue runner runs broader repository verification after EXEC and it fails outside this task's allowed edit surface, do not fix unrelated files; halt according to the conditions below.
+
+## Halt Conditions
+
+The Executor MUST halt without committing and request fresh guidance if any of the following occur:
+
+- Implementing the fix requires editing any file other than `Wait-GitHubActions.ps1` and this dispatch's own allowed handoff/log artifacts.
+- `gh run list --json` in the available `gh` CLI cannot provide `workflowName`; do not substitute a broader GitHub API rewrite without a new Planner packet.
+- The implementation would require changing default workflow discovery, adding CodeQL to defaults, editing `.github/workflows/**`, or broadening the expected workflow set beyond explicit user input.
+- The focused CodeQL verification command exits non-zero after the implementation.
+- Preserving branch filtering, commit filtering, latest-run selection, timeout enforcement, or documented exit-code meanings conflicts with the proposed implementation.
+- The before or after `git status --short --untracked-files=no` check shows tracked changes outside `Wait-GitHubActions.ps1` and this dispatch's own `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-144_*` or `ai_dispatch_logs/log_*.md` artifacts.
+- Any forbidden edit or forbidden artifact is created. Stop immediately, do not expand the diff, and report the deviation in the EXEC report.
+- A correction prompt asks for unrelated source, workflow, CI, docs, test, Cargo, or repository automation changes. Do not execute that correction without a fresh Planner packet.
+
+## Planner Notes
+
+The issue is specifically about the distinction between a run display `name` and the workflow identity `workflowName` returned by `gh run list`. The expected fix is small: request `workflowName`, match explicit expected names against both fields, and keep the rest of the waiter semantics intact.
+
+At planning time, both local `main` and `origin/main` resolve to `42b2bceb980c552c3e7c705e99fe3bc4ec79acea`; use that SHA for the required CodeQL verification command above. The command is intentionally explicit about `-WorkflowName CodeQL` so the task does not make CodeQL part of the default workflow set.
+
